ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general egg tart entry (FOODON_03315091); no specific caramel croissant tart entry exists. Closest parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FJP covers pastry tart/flan category; no caramel croissant tart-specific code exists. Closest defensible category anchor used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for caramel croissant tart or egg tart as a food product in the clinical terminology system.

serving.natural_language_portion: One caramel croissant tart typically weighs approximately 80 g and constitutes a single-serve dessert or snack portion.

nutrient_source.title: Caramel Croissant Tart — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: All per-100g nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Caramel Croissant Tart (cr_id G00010089) with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from analogous croissant/egg tart composition data in USDA FoodData Central (SR Legacy #18231 Danish pastry, #18033 Croissant) as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this item; documented in notes.

gi_source.title: Croissant / butter pastry GI analogous estimate — University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS et al. (2021) International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values 2021, Am J Clin Nutr
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for caramel croissant tart. GI estimated at 67 based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for croissant/butter pastry (GI ~67, medium category). Caramel filling adds rapidly absorbed sugars consistent with this estimate. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (67 × 45.8 × 0.80) / 100 = (67 × 36.64) / 100 ≈ 24.5, rounded to 24.9 using serving-adjusted carbohydrate of 36.64g per 80g serve.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 300 mg/100g combined with high saturated fat (~11.5g/100g), high sugar (11.26g/100g), and low potassium (118 mg/100g) creating an unfavourable sodium-to-potassium ratio
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this item to very occasional small portions, as the combination of 300 mg sodium, high saturated fat, and minimal potassium offset significantly elevates cardiovascular and blood pressure risk.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High carbohydrate load (45.8g/100g) with sugar at 11.26g/100g, estimated medium-high GI (~67), and low dietary fibre (2.6g/100g) producing rapid postprandial glucose elevation
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this item or restrict to very small occasional portions, as the refined croissant pastry and caramel filling create a high glycaemic load (GL ~25 per tart) with rapid glucose absorption and minimal fibre buffering.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Estimated saturated fat ~11.5g/100g (exceeding the 5g/100g threshold) plus trans fat ~0.1g/100g from croissant pastry, cholesterol 67mg/100g, and sodium 300mg/100g collectively elevating LDL-C and cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid this item, as the high saturated fat from butter and cream, trace trans fat from croissant pastry, and elevated sodium together promote LDL cholesterol elevation and endothelial dysfunction.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High saturated fat (~11.5g/100g) and trans fat (~0.1g/100g) promote pro-inflammatory cytokines (TNF-alpha, IL-6); caramel sugar promotes advanced glycation end products (AGEs) worsening joint inflammation
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this item, as the saturated fat, trans fat, and caramel-derived AGEs collectively promote systemic inflammation and may worsen joint symptoms.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Caramel Croissant Tart (焦糖可颂蛋挞) is a Singaporean bakery dessert that fuses the French croissant tradition with the classic Chinese egg tart. The outer shell is made from hand-rolled, laminated puff pastry in the croissant style, baked fresh daily to achieve a distinctly flaky, crisp, and buttery crust. The filling is a smooth, rich custard made from pasteurized egg yolks, pure whole milk, and dairy cream, delivering a pronounced egg-and-milk flavour with a silky, set texture. A homemade caramel sauce is incorporated to add depth, sweetness, and a slightly bitter caramelized note that elevates the overall flavour profile beyond a standard egg tart. Per 100g, the tart provides 406 kcal, 8.2g protein, 21g fat (estimated saturated fat ~11.5g), and 45.8g carbohydrate with 11.26g sugar and 2.6g dietary fibre. Sodium is 300mg/100g. The food is calorie-dense and high in saturated fat and refined carbohydrates, making it a discretionary treat. It carries a medium-to-high glycaemic index (~67) and a high glycaemic load per serving (~25), making it unsuitable for regular consumption by individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. Commonly consumed as a snack or teatime treat in Singapore, it is sold in Chinese bakeries and dessert shops. Allergens include gluten, eggs, milk, and butter.

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient and micronutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010089) with a reported confidence of 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central analogous croissant/pastry entries and documented. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous croissant entry and documented. All sources are approved under Project Veritas v2 source policy.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (11.5g/100g) is an estimate derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for croissant (#18033) and Danish pastry (#18231), as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this item. This estimate is consistent with the known butter and cream content of the recipe. 2. Trans fat (0.1g/100g) is an estimate based on typical croissant pastry composition from USDA FoodData Central; HPB does not report trans fat for this item. 3. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown not available in HPB Foodo database for this item, and USDA analogous entries do not provide a sufficiently close match to justify specific values. 4. water_g set to -1: moisture content not reported in HPB Foodo for this item. 5. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg set to -1: micronutrient detail beyond the HPB-reported fields (potassium, calcium, magnesium, iron, cholesterol) is not available in the HPB Foodo source for this item. 6. GI of 67 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database croissant/butter pastry entry; no direct GI measurement for caramel croissant tart has been published. 7. Halal status is not confirmed; the item contains dairy and egg but halal certification of the specific bakery is not documented in the HPB source. 8. The cultural classification as Chinese-influenced reflects the egg tart tradition originating from Cantonese dim sum culture, adapted in Singapore with a French croissant pastry shell.
